question: How many novels did Agatha Christie publish in her lifetime?
funFact: Agatha Christie's works have sold over two billion copies, making her the best-selling fiction writer of all time after Shakespeare.

question: What is the name of Agatha Christie's famous Belgian detective?
funFact: Poirot is one of only two fictional characters to receive an obituary on the front page of The New York Times.

question: In which year was Agatha Christie born?
funFact: Christie was born in Torquay, Devon, England, on 15 September 1890.

question: Which Agatha Christie novel holds the record as the longest-running play in the West End?
funFact: The Mousetrap opened in London in 1952 and is still running today, making it the world's longest-running theatre production.

question: On which island does the plot of "And Then There Were None" take place?
funFact: "And Then There Were None" is the best-selling mystery novel of all time, with over 100 million copies sold.
